results.tagline: Braised chipotle tofu, tomatillo heat, corn salsa brightness — the Williamsburg plant-forward Mexican bowl.
ingredients: sofritas (organic braised tofu, chipotle, roasted poblano)
corn salsa (corn, poblano, red onion, jalapeño, lime)
guacamole (avocado, lime, cilantro, red onion, jalapeño)
results.trend_signal: al pastor rising — recent_4w 13.2 (US-NY); adobo sauce rising — recent_4w 3.0
results.gap_rationale: Ensenada (mariscos/mezcal, N 4th St) owns the coastal Mexican lane with crispy fish tacos and tostadas. Chipotle can't do fried fish but sofritas + tomatillo + corn salsa delivers the smoky, chile-forward Mexican flavor that Williamsburg's Mexican-curious crowd wants without faking the coastal format.
results.confidence_reason: All pantry items confirmed. Confidence MID because sofritas is not a new product — the insight is framing it against Williamsburg's Mexican scene (Ensenada) rather than inventing a new build. The dish is real and authentic, but not a breakthrough concept.

results.tagline: Slow-roasted pork over rice, tomatillo red chili, corn salsa — chasing Williamsburg's braised meat obsession.
ingredients: tomatillo red chili salsa (hot — dried red chile, chipotle, tomato)
results.gap_rationale: Kokomo (braised oxtail, jerk chicken) and Ensenada together prove Williamsburg diners want slow-cooked, deeply flavored proteins. Birria demand is rising (40.0 recent_4w) but Chipotle can't do consommé. Carnitas with tomatillo red chili salsa — a hot, dried-chile-forward sauce — is the honest pivot: slow-cooked pork with genuine dried chile heat, not a fake birria.
results.confidence_reason: Pantry fully confirmed. MID confidence because carnitas + tomatillo red is a natural Chipotle build, not a novel LTO. The trend framing adds signal but the dish itself won't read as differentiated without intentional positioning.

missing_ingredients: scotch bonnet peppers (non-negotiable jerk heat — habanero is different)
allspice (ground and whole — the defining jerk spice)
fresh thyme (jerk marinade backbone — carnitas has thyme but as slow-roast herb, not jerk marinade)
brown sugar (jerk balance element)
soy sauce or Worcestershire (jerk brine base)
green onion / scallion in marinade
results.local_evidence: Kokomo (Caribbean, Williamsburg) is packed every weekend on braised oxtail and jerk chicken. Jerk is the #1 trending flavor term in the NY trends file (recent_4w 88.2, avg_12m 84.8, peak 100). Chipotle's habanero salsa is superficially hot but scotch bonnet + allspice + thyme as a marinade system is a completely different flavor — fruit-forward heat with warm spice, not just capsaicin.
results.solution: A jerk marinade SKU requires at minimum: scotch bonnet mash, allspice, brown sugar, thyme. Could be added as a protein marinade option (jerk chicken) alongside current adobo prep. Two to three new spice/pepper SKUs needed.

missing_ingredients: oxtail (completely different protein cut — not stocked)
goat meat (not stocked)
curry powder blend (scotch bonnet, allspice, thyme, turmeric, coriander)
scotch bonnet (again)
Jamaican browning sauce
kidney beans in curry sauce (not the current black beans or pinto preparation)
results.local_evidence: Kokomo's braised oxtail is a defining menu item — customers specifically cite it in Yelp reviews as the reason to go. Oxtail trending at recent_4w 22.2, curry goat at 7.0 (recent spike). These are entirely different proteins and preparations from anything in Chipotle's current infrastructure.
results.solution: This is a fundamental protein infrastructure gap. No single SKU addition closes it. A Caribbean protein addition (oxtail or goat) would require separate protein prep stations, sourcing, and slow-cook infrastructure — a significant operational commitment.

missing_ingredients: Mexican dark chocolate
dried chile depth beyond chipotle/tomatillo (ancho, mulato, pasilla needed)
toasted nuts (pepitas, almonds)
dried fruit (raisin base for mole body)
results.local_evidence: Mole trending at recent_4w 72.8 in NY — nearly as strong as CA. Ensenada's coastal Mexican format raises Mexican flavor expectations in the neighborhood. Same gap as WeHo, same solution pathway.
results.solution: Licensed mole drizzle sauce as a single SKU addition — same recommendation as WeHo. The opportunity is large enough in both markets to justify a national LTO if sourced centrally.

results.tagline: Spiced lamb meatballs, harissa, roasted eggplant — Williamsburg's Israeli dining culture, CAVA-fast.
results.trend_signal: shakshuka rising — recent_4w 11.5, avg_12m 13.2 (US-NY); harissa — recent_4w 10.8
results.gap_rationale: K'Far (Chef Solomonov) and Laser Wolf define the Israeli food moment in Williamsburg — shakshuka, mezze, bold spices. CAVA's lamb meatballs + harissa + roasted eggplant + feta is the fast-casual version of that mezze spread. Shakshuka note: eggs are still missing (flagged in gaps) but this bowl captures the flavor architecture.
results.confidence_reason: Every ingredient confirmed in CAVA pantry. Lamb meatballs are a listed protein. Harissa, eggplant dip, crumbled feta all confirmed. This is CAVA's strongest claim on the Israeli food wave dominating Williamsburg.

results.tagline: Turmeric-spiced braised lamb, hummus, sumac slaw, salt-brined pickles — the Laser Wolf mezze at CAVA speed.
ingredients: braised lamb (garlic, onion, turmeric, chili powder)
results.trend_signal: sumac falling in NY (recent_4w 16.5) but mezze format surging via K'Far + Laser Wolf local presence; tahini recent_4w 28.2
results.gap_rationale: Laser Wolf (James Beard buzz) and Lion's Milk (borek, halloumi) put mezze-format dining at the center of Williamsburg's 2026 food identity. CAVA's braised lamb + hummus + sumac + pita is the format-perfect fast-casual answer. No other brand in this comparison set can execute authentic mezze.
results.confidence_reason: All ingredients confirmed. Braised lamb, hummus, sumac slaw, kalamata olives, pita, tzatziki all in pantry. This is CAVA's most defensible competitive position in Williamsburg.

missing_ingredients: scotch bonnet (CAVA has jalapeño heat but not the fruity scotch bonnet profile)
allspice (not in any CAVA ingredient)
jerk marinade system (scotch bonnet, allspice, thyme, brown sugar, soy)
plantain (trending stable at 23.2 — CAVA has no starchy Caribbean base)
results.local_evidence: Kokomo is packed on weekends — jerk chicken is a neighborhood anchor. Jerk is the #1 trending term in NY data (recent_4w 88.2). CAVA's flavor DNA is Eastern Mediterranean; harissa provides chile heat but allspice is the irreplaceable warm-spice element that separates jerk from everything else in CAVA's pantry.
results.solution: CAVA cannot authentically pivot to Caribbean. The pantry overlap is near-zero. This is a true white space — no solution without a fundamental format expansion. Flag for product team as an adjacent concept opportunity, not a pantry extension.

missing_ingredients: eggs (poached/baked — shakshuka is definitionally egg-in-tomato-sauce; no egg protein in CAVA pantry)
cumin-paprika spiced tomato sauce (distinct from fresh tomato + onion topping)
results.local_evidence: K'Far (Chef Solomonov, Williamsburg) built its entire brunch identity on shakshuka and Jerusalem bagels. Shakshuka is rising in NY search (recent_4w 11.5, trend: rising). CAVA has the sauce architecture (harissa + tomato + onion + oregano) but the egg is definitionally required — without it, it's spiced tomato rice, not shakshuka.
results.solution: Add a soft-cooked or poached egg as a protein option — single SKU. This is the highest-leverage single addition CAVA could make for Williamsburg specifically, given K'Far's influence on neighborhood flavor expectations.

missing_ingredients: miso paste (fermented soybean — no equivalent in CAVA pantry)
dashi or soy-based umami broth
sesame oil (toasted — CAVA's sesame citrus herb has sesame flavor but not the oil itself)
nori or furikake
results.local_evidence: Kru (Modern Thai, Michelin Guide + James Beard semifinalist) shows Williamsburg diners are deeply comfortable with fermented, umami-forward Asian flavors. Miso trending at recent_4w 49.5 in NY. CAVA's sesame citrus herb dressing nods at this flavor family but without miso's fermented depth, it's a different category entirely.
results.solution: Add a miso-tahini dressing blend (miso + existing tahini = one hybrid SKU). Miso and tahini are both fermented-paste condiments — this is the most naturalistic CAVA expansion possible. Unlocks miso grain bowl builds with existing rice bases.

results.tagline: Miso-glazed salmon, crispy rice, nori sesame, spicy cashew — Williamsburg's Japanese umami moment, no reservation required.
results.trend_signal: miso rising — recent_4w 49.5, avg_12m 59.0 (US-NY); yuzu rising — recent_4w 32.0
results.gap_rationale: Kru (Michelin-noted Modern Thai) validates Williamsburg's appetite for umami-forward Asian food. Sweetgreen's Miso Glazed Salmon + crispy rice combination creates a poke-adjacent bowl that speaks directly to this trend. The crispy rice from the Crispy Rice Bowl menu item is the differentiating element — it adds texture that no competitor can match in this format.
results.confidence_reason: Miso glazed salmon confirmed. Crispy rice confirmed (from Crispy Rice Bowl). Nori sesame seasoning confirmed. All elements verified in sweetgreen_menu.json. This is a mashup of two existing dishes — zero pantry gaps.

results.tagline: Caramelized garlic steak, hummus, cucumber, lime cilantro heat — Sweetgreen's answer to the Israeli dining moment.
results.gap_rationale: K'Far and Laser Wolf have made mezze the defining food identity of Williamsburg in 2026. Sweetgreen already has a confirmed Steak Mezze bowl on the menu — this is their direct entry point into the Israeli dining wave. The lime cilantro jalapeño sauce adds a brightness twist that differentiates it from CAVA's purer Mediterranean play.
results.confidence_reason: Steak Mezze is a confirmed existing Sweetgreen dish in sweetgreen_menu.json. All ingredients verified. No pantry gaps. Framing against Williamsburg's Israeli restaurant wave (K'Far, Laser Wolf) is the market-fit argument.

missing_ingredients: scotch bonnet (no equivalent in Sweetgreen pantry — jalapeño ranch is a different heat profile)
allspice
jerk marinade for protein (no current chicken prep uses Caribbean spicing)
plantain (no starchy Caribbean base)
results.local_evidence: Jerk is the #1 trend signal in Williamsburg (recent_4w 88.2, Kokomo packed weekends). Sweetgreen's blackened chicken uses a Cajun-adjacent spice rub — superficially similar but fundamentally different from jerk's allspice-scotch bonnet backbone. The Sweetgreen hot sauce provides generic heat, not jerk's characteristic warm-spice fruity heat.
results.solution: A jerk-marinated chicken option would require a new protein prep: scotch bonnet, allspice, thyme, brown sugar marinade. Could slot into the Blackened Chicken prep lane operationally. Two to three new spice SKUs needed. This is a significant but realistic LTO addition given Sweetgreen's precedent with blackened and KBBQ formats.

missing_ingredients: oxtail protein (not stocked, no analog)
Jamaican curry / browning sauce
slow-braise infrastructure for collagen-rich cuts
results.local_evidence: Kokomo's braised oxtail is the neighborhood's signature plate. Oxtail trending at recent_4w 22.2. Sweetgreen's protein model (roasted, blackened, grilled, sous vide) has no slow-braise format — this is an operational gap, not just a missing ingredient.
results.solution: Not achievable within current Sweetgreen operational model. Sweetgreen's proteins are all quick-cook or pre-prepped. Oxtail requires 4–6 hour braising. This is a concept-level gap, not a SKU fix — flag for innovation pipeline, not near-term LTO.

missing_ingredients: eggs (no egg protein in any Sweetgreen dish)
spiced tomato sauce base (Sweetgreen has tomatoes as topping, not as warm braised sauce)
results.local_evidence: K'Far's shakshuka defines the Williamsburg brunch moment. Sweetgreen's Hummus Crunch bowl approaches Mediterranean territory but without eggs, it cannot claim the shakshuka trend. Sweetgreen has hummus, feta, and pesto vinaigrette — enough for a mezze-adjacent dish but not a shakshuka.
results.solution: Add soft-cooked egg as a protein option. This is a cross-market recommendation (same as CAVA Williamsburg gap). For Sweetgreen specifically, a warm tomato-harissa sauce cup would also be needed to make the shakshuka format legible — two SKU additions.
